items.title: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s.

The 2030 swap with the Heat could potentially offset the swap they already owe to the Trail Blazers.

They could really use more second-round picks.

items.title: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade.

They could trade their 2029 first-round pick on the condition that their 2027 first-round pick (top-14 protected) conveys to Charlotte.

They could try to regain control of it in a trade with the Hornets.

items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team Giannis Antetokounmpo trade framework.

They'd create a massive $25.5 million trade exception, which could grow if they expand the trade.

It would be useful for taking on bad salaries with draft picks to add to their surplus.

items.title: Milwaukee got a lot but I have no idea if that Miami deal scares who it needs to. Knicks scared? No. Celtics? Nah - maybe anger them b/c apparently they had a proposed deal per Shams. Cleveland and Detroit? Eye don't think so
items.author: Evan Barnes
items.body_excerpt:
Milwaukee got a lot but I have no idea if that Miami deal scares who it needs to. Knicks scared? No. Celtics? Nah - maybe anger them b/c apparently they had a proposed deal per Shams. Cleveland and Detroit? Eye don't think so

Let's see what happens when the ball rolls out.

items.title: "It's done, it's dead" (c) Doechii.
items.author: Evan Barnes
items.body_excerpt:
"It's done, it's dead" (c) Doechii. 

No more trade rumors or speculation. My Bucks and Heat colleagues can breathe again like Toni Braxton...and get ready for another wild chapter.

items.title: This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now.

They'll need to reduce their payroll to create space to re-sign him.

They could also use more flexibility to open up the full $15M mid-level.

Maybe the Celtics & Brown smooth this over. Their pursuit of Kevin Durant in 2022 & Brown's name living in the rumor mill hasn't led to a break up yet.

items.author: Dalton Johnson
items.body_excerpt: We’re all winners for the Giannis headache being over

items.title: The Giannis Antetokounmpo saga ends 418 days after the intial report of his openness to a trade.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Giannis Antetokounmpo saga ends 418 days after the intial report of his openness to a trade.

One of the most drawn-out trade sagas in NBA history that tops the Dwightmare, Melodrama, Paul George, Kawhi Leonard, Ben Simmons, James Harden (LAC) & Kevin Durant (PHO & HOU).

items.title: Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per year, higher than I think a lot of folks thought it might. One thing about the Connelly era -- a few more million than perceived isn't much for him to haggle over if he thinks you're a part of the core long term.
items.author: Chris Hine
items.body_excerpt: Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per year, higher than I think a lot of folks thought it might. One thing about the Connelly era -- a few more million than perceived isn't much for him to haggle over if he thinks you're a part of the core long term.

items.title: Also: an absolute lock that there will be an important Pacers-Heat game(s) this season.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Also: an absolute lock that there will be an important Pacers-Heat game(s) this season.

items.title: The Heat are now hard capped to the first apron by using the 125 percent expanded trade exception for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Heat are now hard capped to the first apron by using the 125 percent expanded trade exception for Giannis Antetokounmpo.

They have $15.6 million in first apron space to fill 4 roster spots.

They can create more space by trading Nikola Jovic or if Andrew Wiggins opts out.

The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will

items.title: Fever overcame 2/18 shooting in the 1st, then were +32 in the middle two quarters and held on through a physical 44-min 4Q to beat the Mercury.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever overcame 2/18 shooting in the 1st, then were +32 in the middle two quarters and held on through a physical 44-min 4Q to beat the Mercury.

Clark: 24p, 9a
Monique Billings: 14/10
Six technicals. Plenty of tension. Rematch Wednesday.

www.fieldhousefiles.com/p/fever-merc...

media.title: Fever shake off ugly start, outlast Mercury in physical, emotional finish
media.description: The Indiana Fever overcame an awful first quarter and a physical, tension-filled fourth to beat the Phoenix Mercury 86-77 on Tuesday night at Gainbridge Fieldhouse.

items.title: Updated Wolves cap situation after re-signing Ayo Dosunmu.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Updated Wolves cap situation after re-signing Ayo Dosunmu.

His $22.4 million annual salary likely means a cap space team was lurking to give him a salary above the $15 million mid-level.

They are $31 million below the first apron. Question is if they stay below that or the tax.
